expressions.english: breakfast
supper
fried rice
bibimbap; mixed rice
to eat; to have a meal
to skip a meal
expressions.notes: 굶다 = to skip a meal; to starve

expressions.english: to cook; to make steamed rice
expressions.notes: 밥을 하다 = to cook a meal; to make food; to cook steamed rice

expressions.english: Did you have breakfast?
Do you want to go eat?
Let's go for a walk after eating.
expressions.notes:
산책 = walk
산책 가다 = to go for a walk

expressions.english: Give me one more bowl of rice, please.
expressions.notes:
한 is the adjective form of 하나.
그릇 = dish, bowl

expressions.english: Where are you going in the middle of a meal?
expressions.notes: -다 말고 = while + S + is in the middle of + V-ing

expressions.english: I had some bibimbap for dinner.
expressions.notes: 비빔밥 = steamed rice mixed with vegetables (and sometimes meat, depending on the type) with red pepper paste

expressions.english: I haven't eaten, so I am hungry.
expressions.notes:
배 = stomach, belly
배가 고프다 = to be hungry

expressions.english: Shall we meet up and eat together next week?
expressions.notes:
다음 = next
주 = week
같이 = together; with + N
-(으)ㄹ까요? = Shall we + S + V?; Do you want me to + V?; Should we + V?; Let's + V.

expressions.korean: 밥 너무 많이 먹는 거 아니에요?
expressions.english: Aren't you eating too much?

expressions.korean: 배 고프죠? 제가 지금 밥 할게요.
expressions.english: You must be hungry! I will make some food for you now.

expressions.korean: 너무 바빠서 밥 먹는 것도 잊어버렸어요.
expressions.english: I was very busy, so I even forgot to eat.
expressions.notes:
바빠다 = to be busy
잊어버리다 = to forget

expressions.korean: 다이어트 하느라 하루 종일 밥을 굶었어요.
expressions.english: I am on a diet, so I skipped meals all day long.
expressions.notes:
다이어트 하다 = to be on a diet
-느라 = for + V-ing, while + V-ing
하루 = one day
하루 종일 = all day long

expressions.korean: 밥 먹을 때에는 텔레비전을 꺼면 안 될까요?
expressions.english: Can we turn off the television while we are eating?
expressions.notes:
텔레비전 = television
꺼다 = to turn off
-(으)면 안 될까? = Can/Can't we ...?
